Tech.title: Health data giant CareCloud says hackers accessed patients’ medical records
Tech.summary: CareCloud, a major provider of medical records storage, said hackers accessed one of its repositories of patient data earlier in March. It provides technnology for more than 45,000 providers covering millions of patients.

Tech.title: Rivian spinoff Also will build autonomous delivery vehicles for DoorDash
Tech.summary: DoorDash joins Greenoaks Capital in another $200 million funding round for Also, bringing its total funding to more than $500 million.

Tech.title: Roku launches a standalone app for Howdy, its $2.99 streaming service
Tech.summary: With this new app, Roku says subscribers can access Howdy's library of content on the go.

Tech.title: Meta launches two new Ray-Ban glasses designed for prescription wearers
Tech.summary: Meta says these glasses are the most comfortable ones it has ever designed, as they're made for all-day comfort.

Tech.title: The company behind ClassPass and Mindbody just got a lot bigger with a $7.5B merger
Tech.summary: The merger is a sign that the fitness industry is continuing to move toward consolidation to compete at a larger scale. Recent moves include MyFitnessPal acquiring Cal AI, an AI calorie counting app, and Strava buying two apps: cycling app The Breakaway and running app Runna.

Tech.title: Meta agrees to ‘reduce’ Instagram’s PG-13 rating references
Tech.summary: Meta has reached an agreement with the Motion Picture Association (MPA) that will require the platform to "substantially reduce" its reference to the PG-13 rating when describing Instagram's teen accounts. Tech.title: OkCupid settles claims it shared user photos with a facial recognition company
Tech.summary: Dating app OkCupid agreed to settle claims from the Federal Trade Commission that it deceived millions of users by sharing their photos with a third-party facial recognition company without their consent. OkCupid and parent company Match Group did not admit wrongdoing as part of the settlement, but

Tech.title: Instagram tests letting you pay to view stories anonymously
Tech.summary: Meta is testing an Instagram subscription that offers a bunch of new perks, such as the ability to view stories without the poster knowing, according to a report from TechCrunch.
